cit.basis: Flat 28% CIT, reduced from 30% by the 2023 Income Tax Law (Law No 027/2022), administered by the Rwanda Revenue Authority (RRA).

roadmap.note: The government's Medium-Term Revenue Strategy foresees a further reduction of the CIT rate from 28% to 26%.

small_business_regimes.note: Micro and small businesses below the real-regime turnover thresholds may opt for flat-tax / lump-sum regimes instead of the standard 28% CIT; out of scope for this SME estimator.

newly_listed_incentives.note: Newly listed companies on the Rwanda Stock Exchange enjoy reduced CIT rates of 25% or 20% for a set period, depending on the share of equity floated to the public; out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: VAT at 18%; registration threshold and zero-rated/exempt supplies apply.

personal_income_tax.note: Progressive PAYE with a top rate of 30%; out of scope for this CIT estimator.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Small-business flat/lump-sum regimes, listing incentives, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official RRA guidance and a local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
